figures.caption: Table 1. Summary of bias properties, computational requirements, and training time.

Table 2. Mean RL2 errors (×10−2) with standard deviations (×10−2) over three random seeds. The best and second-best results are highlighted in bold with underline and bold, respectively. For AC, the error is computed only at t = 0.
Figure 2. Time-resolved relative error on the BSB benchmark: relative error evaluated at each PDE time t ∈ T using the same models as in Table 2. The y-axis is plotted on a logarithmic scale.
Figure 3. Runtime–accuracy on the BSB benchmark: RL2 recorded over runtime. Both axes are plotted on a log scale.
Figure 8. BSB(d = 100) path prediction with error band in the hard-constraint setting.
Figure 6. HJB(d = 100) path prediction with error band in the hard-constraint setting.
Figure 7. BSB(d = 100) path prediction with error band in the soft-constraint setting.
Figure 4. Relative error over PDE time (left) and RL2 over training runtime (right) for Shotgun and its debiased variant.
Table 3. Performance comparison for M1 = M2 ∈ {1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50} under soft and hard constraints. Values show mean RL2 error (×10−2) with standard deviation (×10−2) over three random seeds.
Figure 5. HJB(d = 100) path prediction with error band in the soft-constraint setting.

Figure 1. Illustration of the one-step loss computation for Un-EM-BSDE with M1 = M2 = 2. The many gray curves visualize the randomness of solution trajectories (possible SDE sample paths). The dashed curves denote a ground-truth trajectory, whereas the solid curves indicate the one-step predicted trajectory. The errors from the two independently formed groups are multiplied to produce an unbiased estimator.
